ira_contribution_limits_2025.content: For tax year 2025, the IRA contribution limit is $7,000 for individuals under age 50, and $8,000 for those age 50 and older. This applies to both Traditional and Roth IRAs combined. Roth IRA eligibility phases out for single filers with modified adjusted gross income between $146,000 and $161,000.

401k_contribution_limits_2025.content: For 2025, the employee contribution limit for 401(k), 403(b), and most 457 plans is $23,500. Catch-up contributions for employees age 50 and older add $7,500. The total combined employee plus employer contribution limit is $70,000.

capital_gains_long_term.content: Long-term capital gains apply to assets held more than one year. For 2025, single filers pay 0% on gains up to $48,350, 15% on gains from $48,351 to $533,400, and 20% above $533,400. Short-term capital gains (assets held one year or less) are taxed as ordinary income.

capital_gains_short_term.content: Short-term capital gains apply to assets sold within one year of purchase. These gains are taxed at the same rates as ordinary income, ranging from 10% to 37% based on the taxpayer's federal income tax bracket.

wash_sale_rule.content: A wash sale occurs when an investor sells a security at a loss and buys the same or a substantially identical security within 30 days before or after the sale. Losses from wash sales are disallowed for tax purposes and added to the cost basis of the replacement security.

student_loan_interest_deduction.content: Taxpayers may deduct up to $2,500 of interest paid on qualified student loans. The deduction phases out for single filers with modified adjusted gross income between $80,000 and $95,000. The loan must have been used for qualified higher education expenses.

f1_opt_tax_status.content: Students on F-1 visas are generally considered nonresident aliens for tax purposes during their first five calendar years in the United States. Nonresident aliens on F-1 OPT are exempt from FICA taxes (Social Security and Medicare). They must file Form 1040-NR rather than Form 1040 during the nonresident alien period.

diversification_guidance.content: A diversified portfolio typically spreads investments across asset classes including stocks, bonds, and alternative investments, as well as across sectors and geographies. Common guidance suggests that no single stock should represent more than 5 to 10 percent of a portfolio, and that single-sector concentration should be monitored carefully.

emergency_fund_guidance.content: Financial guidance commonly recommends maintaining an emergency fund covering three to six months of essential living expenses. Funds should be kept in a liquid, accessible account such as a high-yield savings account separate from regular checking.

duplicate_charge_dispute.content: Consumers have the right to dispute duplicate charges or billing errors on credit card statements within 60 days of the statement date. Card issuers must investigate and respond within 90 days. Documented duplicate charges are typically reversed without dispute.

expense_ratio_guidance.content: Index fund expense ratios typically range from 0.03% to 0.20%. Lower-cost index funds (under 0.10%) include Vanguard's VOO (0.03%) and VTI (0.03%). Actively managed mutual funds often charge 0.50% to 1.50%, which over decades can significantly reduce returns.

anomaly_detection_principles.content: Common transaction anomaly signals include: amounts that deviate significantly from category averages (z-score above 2.5), duplicate charges from the same merchant on the same day, unusual merchant names or unfamiliar vendors, round-number transactions in categories where rounded amounts are uncommon, and rapid succession of similar charges.
